title: Save changes with a direct commit

blocks.content: Saving dashboard changes with a direct commit pushes modifications immediately to the main branch in GitHub. This workflow suits quick updates, trusted changes, or environments where dashboard modifications don't require team review before deployment.
To save changes with a direct commit, complete the following steps:

whenTrue.content: If this guide is floating, dock it to the sidebar first.
Pop this guide out so it stays visible when the **Provisioned dashboard** save drawer opens.

blocks.content: In this section, you commit dashboard changes directly to the main branch.
With the dashboard in edit mode and changes made, click **Save**. The **Provisioned dashboard** drawer opens.
In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, optionally check **Update default refresh value** to make the current refresh interval the new default.
In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, optionally check **Update default variable values** to make the current variable values the new defaults.
In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, optionally confirm **Repository folder**. Leave empty for the repository root.

In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, confirm **Filename**.

For example, `http-monitoring.json`.
In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, enter `main` in **Branch**. This commits changes directly to the main branch without creating a pull request.

blocks.targetvalue: Add error rate visualization to HTTP monitoring dashboard
blocks.content:
In the **Provisioned dashboard** drawer, optionally enter a **Comment** describing your changes.

For example, enter `Add error rate visualization to HTTP monitoring dashboard`.

blocks.tooltip: Pushes your dashboard changes to the main branch.

blocks.content: The dashboard changes are committed to the main branch in GitHub. Git Sync creates a commit with your message and pushes it to the repository. The dashboard is updated in both Grafana and GitHub.

whenTrue.content: Dock this guide back to the sidebar to continue to the next milestone.

blocks.content: In the next milestone, you'll learn how to save changes using a pull request workflow.
